explanation.one: As production complexity increased and deadlines tightened, the need for efficient teamwork became more crucial. The growing amount of data to manage made it harder to deliver high-quality movies, often keeping people late at night. That's why we decided to put an end to this. We wake up every morning with one goal: to help you deliver high-end visuals while keeping the process enjoyable.
explanation.two: To achieve this, we reimagined communication both inside and outside the studio. Internally, we transformed the production tracker into a collaborative platform where all stakeholders can share information in real time and become more autonomous. Externally, we enabled talents from different studios to connect and grow their skills together.
explanation.three: Through our solutions, artists can collaborate effortlessly with teams and can focus on their craft, developing their skills and reaching new heights. Meanwhile, studios can scale seamlessly, unleashing their potential and fulfilling their ambitions.

one.title: Simplicity
one.text: The lighter our tools, the more agile we become. It clears our minds and speeds everything up.

two.title: Collaboration
two.text: When ideas and work are shared, everyone wins. We learn faster and achieve more.

three.title: Craftsmanship
three.text: Well-made things last longer. When they are a joy to work on, it leads to better software.

one.title: Open Company
one.text: We share most of what we build and learn. We have also made our KPIs public.

two.title: Remote
two.text: We work from where we want when we want. Only productivity matters.

three.title: Deliberate Practicing
three.text: We always need to learn. That's why we dedicate one day a week to self-training.

team.title: Who we are
team.intro: We are a passionate team based in France with extensive experience in software development, production management, and product development.

frank.name: Frank Rousseau
frank.description: Before CGWire, Frank worked for studios and co-founded a startup. He has 15 years of experience in tech companies. Today, he nurtures the CGWire and Kitsu brands and gathers the right people to build them.

gwen.name: Gwenaëlle Dupré
gwen.description: Gwenaëlle has over 10 years of experience as a production manager with distributed teams. At CGWire, she handles all product management and sales aspects, ensuring the success of every Kitsu implementation.

nicolas.name: Nicolas Ledez
nicolas.description: Nicolas is one of the most renowned French System Administrators and a speaker at many tech events. He manages our cloud and on-premise infrastructures, ensuring your Kitsu instance is always up and running.

nicolaspennec.name: Nicolas Pennec
nicolaspennec.description: Nicolas is an experienced JavaScript developer with 15 years of experience at companies such as OVH and Orange. He frequently speaks at conferences about frontend technologies and is in charge of all Kitsu UIs.

evan.name: Evan Blaudy
evan.description: Evan is an experienced Python Software Engineer involved in open-source projects. At CGWire, he maintains and improves the Kitsu API and DCC integrations.

elsa.name: Elsa Chevallier
elsa.description: Elsa has worked for 15 years in the movie industry as a Chief Financial Officer in studios and organizations promoting cinema. She is responsible for the financial aspects at CGWire.

design.title: Website illustrations
design.information: All artworks are created by the Naolito Studio and Ev Style. They are licensed under Creative Commons by-nc-sa.

blender.information: All production images shown in screenshots were created by Blender Studio during the production of Sprite Fright. They are licensed under Creative Commons.

carbonReport.title: Carbon Report
carbonReport.subtitle: Our environmental footprint
carbonReport.explanationOne: At CGWire, we measured the real impact of our activity on the environment. This 2024 report highlights our main emission posts, our progress margins, and the concrete actions we are committed to to reduce our footprint.

consumption.title: Our carbon consumption
consumption.explanation: Our business trips by plane represent 50% of our total emissions. To better observe the distribution of other categories, you can remove them from the calculations.

categories.title: Our breakdown by categories
categories.explanation: The results show the distribution of our emissions, with the transport with the trip to Japan representing more than 50% of our total emissions.

evolution.title: Our evolution tracks to reduce our impact
evolution.cloudTwo: Reduce the weight of media (images, videos, etc.)
evolution.cloudThree: Prefer French servers powered by low-carbon energy
evolution.foodOne: Reduce plane trips to the strict minimum
evolution.foodTwo: Responsible catering: encourage employees to eat vegetarian more often
evolution.foodThree: Prefer high-quality equipment for longer conservation

anticipation.title: Our projects for 2026: Anticipate to reduce our impact
anticipation.warning: +57% potential emissions in 2026 if no action is taken

conclusion.title: We are a company in the cultural and creative industries sector
conclusion.explanation: We allow studios to collaborate better to deliver better results. To make this contribution sustainable, we strive to apply the best practices of community life on all fronts: open-source software, transparent accounting, and environmental impact analysis. The goal is to include our company as much as possible in our society and our environment.

community.subtitle: Join your peers
community.explanationOne: As we progressed in building CGWire, we increasingly felt the need to enhance collaboration between studios. That's why we have fostered both online and offline communities of animation professionals. Topics extend beyond just Kitsu. TDs and production managers share tips and best practices to tackle their day-to-day challenges. Below, you'll find the platform we use to connect with our community.

chat.subtitle: Discord
chat.title: Connect with your peers
chat.explanation: Join our Discord channel. Industry professionals share and exchange best practices about Kitsu and animation/VFX pipelines!

github.subtitle: Github
github.title: Contribute to Kitsu
github.explanation: Post bug reports or feature requests on our GitHub repositories. See how we code, participate in architecture discussions, and even better, send your pull requests!

feedback.subtitle: Canny
feedback.title: Give feedback
feedback.explanation: Post feedback and comments. Vote for existing feature requests. See what is under development and help shape the future of Kitsu with the community.

translate.subtitle: POEditor
translate.title: Translate Kitsu
translate.explanation: Kitsu is already available in 6 languages. With your help, we can go further. Translate Kitsu in your language and allow more people to access our open-source technologies.

quotes.tant-mieux: Communication with remote studios have been simplified. We can discuss a specific asset or task without exchanging 10 emails. If we have any doubts, we can rely on what is indicated on the platform.
quotes.miyu: A few days are enough to understand all the features required to run a production. Everyone in the studio starts quickly, especially artists who really enjoy Kitsu. We didn't notice this enthusiasm with the previous tools we used.
quotes.fost: With Kitsu, people think first about the time saved but what matters more to me is the serenity you get by having everything in order. It gave you more space in your brain to focus on other elements of your production.
quotes.adm: We needed a tool to link tasks and visual deliveries. We wanted practical and pleasant software for the teams. Especially for Filmmakers, who had to access the shots end-to-end at any moment. And of course, all while allowing production managers to track the progress with ease.
quotes.makuta: Kitsu has pretty much unified everything and is forcing neater communication not only between artists but from production, management, and clients.
quotes.ryff: Kitsu was straightforward. It was simple. You didn't need a degree in computer science like you do with alternatives.
quotes.remembers: I knew that if I brought in a new tool, I needed to address a need that hadn't been identified by the core team already in place. The main challenge right away was not to constrain them.

main.title: We Connect Creative Minds
main.subtitle: CGWire develops Kitsu, a platform for animation, VFX, and video game studios that brings teams and tools together in a single place. Collaborate, manage deliveries, and perform reviews from anywhere.
main.tagline: Elevate Your Productions To The Next Level

explanation.one: With Kitsu, you can enable efficient collaboration between distributed teams, whether in the studio or from home. This results in better decision-making and faster deliveries.

text.one: Receive up-to-date information on your production's progress, allowing you to assign tasks and provide efficient feedback, keeping your team on track.
text.two: Your content is stored securely and will always be accessible to your team and throughout your pipeline. Everyone knows what to do, whether in the studio or from home.
text.three: Streamlined and intuitive software is quicker to use and easier to get started with. With Kitsu, you can start your projects without the need for a consultant or intensive training.
text.four: Kitsu saves time and increases efficiency. Once integrated into your pipeline, it supercharges your studio. Managing productions is easier than ever!

conclusion.tagline: Harnessing our technical expertise to empower your creativity
conclusion.text: CGWire's software is open source and developed in collaboration with studios. All features and functionality have been validated through real-life use cases.

scale.forecastText: Test scenarios and see how your production evolves.
scale.todosText: Empower your artists with efficient to-do lists.
scale.reviewsText: Conduct reviews for each delivery and compare different versions.
scale.reportsText: Have a 360° view on your productions and stay in control.
scale.apiText: Integrate production data into your pipeline.
scale.storiesText: Discover how studios have used our products to enhance collaboration and successfully complete productions.

stats.title: Trusted by 300+ studios
stats.text: Creatives from all over the world use Kitsu to manage their productions.

kitsu.title: Give Superpowers To Your Teams
kitsu.description: Kitsu is a collaboration platform for animation, VFX, and game studios. It empowers your teams to deliver higher-quality content.

modules.subtitle: Designed with production use cases in mind

2d.title: Kitsu for 2D Animation
2d.content: 2D Animation productions require to deliver a lot of shots in a short amount of time while keeping a high quality. With Kitsu, you can schedule, dispatch and review your shots easily. That way, you can deliver your best work on time.

3d.title: Kitsu for 3D Animation
3d.content: 3D Animation productions require to deliver complex shots involving a lot of assets and iterations. With Kitsu, you can clarify the chaos. Your teams can focus on delivering the best work possible.

vfx.title: Kitsu for VFX
vfx.content: VFX productions involves a lot of modified shots requiring many iterations and handled by distributed teams. With Kitsu, you can organize your production in a way to avoid any confusion and waste of time. Your teams can be fully productive.

games.title: Kitsu for Video Games
games.content: Video Games productions require to deal with a lot of in-game assets while creating cinematics. Artists are hired all around the world and need to be able to collaborate easily. With Kitsu, it's easy to keep track of all the assets and iterations and make sure everyone is on the same page.

schools.title: Kitsu for Schools
schools.content: Schools needs to create a bridge between the school and the studios. With Kitsu, students can work on their projects in a frame similar to the industry. They can express their artistic vision while being team players. Teachers can follow their progress and provide feedback efficiently.

services.subtitle: An outstanding user experience
services.title: Robust services to support your production
services.description: A production database thrives with the right support. Our industry-experienced support team is always there for you, ensuring everything runs smoothly and securely.

stories.subtitle: Testimonials
stories.title: Read our customer stories
stories.description: See how our customers implemented Kitsu in their studios for any kind of productions.

api.content: Use our Python API to integrate Kitsu into your tools. Pull data from Kitsu and update it from your tools.
api.title: Python API

assets.content: Organize your assets and keep track of their versions. Assign related tasks to your team and follow their progress.
assets.title: Asset Management

breakdown.content: Kitsu allows you to list which asset belongs to which shot. It gives you extra statistics about each shot ready status
breakdown.title: Breakdown List

forecast.content: Test scenarios and see how current costs compare to your budget previsions. Anticipate any issues and make sure you stay on track.
forecast.title: Budget Forecasting

timesheets.content: Let artists track their time and get a detailed overview of your how much time each artist has worked. Aggregate data on assets and shots.
timesheets.title: Time tracking

news.content: Follow the activity of your production in real-time. Subscribe to key tasks and be notified when their status change.
news.title: Activity feed

report.content: Via dashboards, you can build reports about your production progress. Your partners will know exactly where you are and what to do.
report.title: Production Reports

review.content: Create playlists to review your shots and assets consecutively. Provide feedback efficiently and keep access to all preview versions to compare with earlier deliveries.
review.title: Review engine

review-assets.content: Create playlists to review your assets consecutively. Provide feedback efficiently and keep access to all preview versions to compare with earlier deliveries.
review-assets.title: Review engine

schedule.content: Kitsu helps you manage your workload. You can schedule your tasks, assign them to artists, and balance them efficiently.
schedule.title: Schedule and planning

search.content: Quickly find assets by searching for keywords. Use tags and metadata to allow for more advanced searches.
search.title: Search engine

track.content: List and dispatch tasks for all your steps. Set statuses and run discussion directly with your team.
track.title: Task tracking

library.content: Build your own library of assets and reuse them in your shots and assets. Avoid duplicating assets and keep your team on the same page.
library.title: Asset library

supporters.content: Productions tracked with Kitsu won all the major awards: Oscars, Emmys, Cannes Palme d'Or, Annecy Cristal, César, and more.
supporters.games: Video Games productions tracked with Kitsu are among the most played in the world.
supporters.schools: The best French animation schools already use Kitsu to train their students.
supporters.subtitle: Fullfill your ambitions
supporters.title: Kitsu is the biggest supporter of your success
supporters.vfx: VFX Productions tracked with Kitsu met great success in theaters. We will back you up to achieve the same.

pricing.title: Kitsu Cloud plans for studios of all sizes

cloud.explanation: We provide a cloud installation with user support, trainings, extra features, plugins deployment, and contractor synchronization.

contact.explanation: We can adapt our offer to your specific needs and propose professional services

discount.details: All plans come with a two weeks trial

faq.answer1: We accept credit cards only for monthly payments. For yearly subscriptions, you can pay via credit card, wire transfer or with a SEPA payment.
faq.answer10: Monthly subscriptions can be canceled through your account page. For yearly subscription please contact our team, and we will set the cancelation in our billing system.
faq.answer11: Yes, we can provide tailor-made plans that fit your production.
faq.answer2: All the API features are included in your subscription: REST routes, event stream, and full-text search.
faq.answer3: Every feature is included in the free trial with unlimited user access. But Kitsu will run on low-end hardware. To try it with stronger hardware please contact our team.
faq.answer4: It corresponds to your installation of Kitsu. It runs on its own domain name (yourstudio.cg-wire.com). You will have a different account on it and your team will interact with this installation.
faq.answer5: Yes, this is what we name On-Premise hosting. We manage Kitsu on your server like if it was on the cloud.
faq.answer6: We set the disk limitation to make sure you don't feel constrained by. If you have a very intensive load of previews please contact us to estimate extra costs. As a rule of thumb, a 50x11' TV series will require 200GB.
faq.answer7: You can change it at any time. You will pay the remaining period at pro-rata rates. If you downgrade, your subscription a credit note will be submitted for future renewals.
faq.answer8: If you run a monthly subscription, you can change it directly through your account page. For yearly subscriptions, you have to contact our sales team. We will operate the change in our billing system.
faq.answer9: A yearly subscription means that you pay for 12 months upfront. By showing your commitment, you benefit from a 20% discount compared to the monthly rate.
faq.question1: What forms of payment do you accept?
faq.question10: How do I cancel my subscription?
faq.question11: My production will run on X months. Can you provide a subscription for this?
faq.question2: Do I have access to the API with my subscription?
faq.question3: What is included in the free trial?
faq.question5: Can I make Kitsu run on my servers?
faq.question6: Is the disk space provided enough for my needs?
faq.question7: Can I change my subscription once it has started?
faq.question8: How do I change my subscription?

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions

firsteps.cta1: Book a meeting or a demo
firsteps.question1: Schedule a meeting with a member of our team. Together, we'll explore how Kitsu can improve your workflow and empower your business.
firsteps.question2: Then join our Discord community to get in touch with other users and talk about pipelines and workflows.
firsteps.question3: Register to access your Kitsu Cloud installation free for two weeks. All features are included, allowing you to test it's full potential.

opensource.details: Kitsu is free and open-source, allowing your team to freely use and modify it on your infrastructure.

pricing.title: Pricing

usecase.agency: For shorts, commercials, and light productions
usecase.production: For TV series, feature films, and video games
usecase.partner: For large studios or TPN compliance

header.subtitle: Connect all your tools
header.metrics: More than 200 000 assets have been managed with Kitsu!
header.explanation: Your data flows throughout your entire pipeline. That's why we provide integrations to facilitate communication between your tools. We enable your artists to publish from their tools, allow your team to send notifications to your chat applications, and offer API clients for extending your workflow.

integrations.titleDcc: Publish from your Favorite Digital Content Creation Tools
integrations.subtitleChats: Keep your team up to date
integrations.titleChats: Push notifications to your chat applications
integrations.subtitleSoftware: Access Kitsu data in your tools
integrations.titleSoftware: Integrate Kitsu directly into Your Code

spreadsheets.title: Build your workflow with our spreadsheets

spreadsheets.tagline: Build your workflow with our spreadsheets
spreadsheets.explanationOne: We understand that you are accustomed to spreadsheets and that transitioning to a new tool can be challenging. Even if you are not ready to use Kitsu, we want to assist you. That's why we've prepared production-ready spreadsheets for you. You can use them as a starting point to build your workflow or keep them throughout your production. Everything is free and licensed under Creative Commons BY-NC-SA 2.0. Enjoy!

studios.title: Creative Studios and Schools
studios.subtitle: Kitsu is used all over the world

header.tagline: Always know which shots are ready to build
header.subtitle: Casting Management
header.metrics: More than 200 000 assets have been managed with Kitsu!
header.explanation: Chasing down casting information across spreadsheets and emails slows your whole pipeline. Kitsu gives every shot a structured breakdown so your scene builders always know exactly what's ready, what's missing, and what to do next.

features.title: List your breakdown
features.subtitle: Stop guessing what belongs in each shot
features.content: Build breakdown tables showing the cast assets for each shot, organized by type. Add metadata directly to every entry so your scene builder has everything they need before they even open a file.

features.title: Link assets together
features.subtitle: No more manually tracking what goes with what
features.content: Connect assets to define how they relate: populate sets, attach props to characters, or model any dependency that fits your workflow.

features.title: Export your casting
features.subtitle: Your pipeline tools shouldn't have to wait on you
features.content: Export casting data as a flat .csv or a column view in seconds. Or skip the manual step entirely and connect your scene builder directly to the API.

features.title: Track shot readiness
features.subtitle: Know what's actually ready
features.content: Get per-shot readiness statistics based on casting completeness. Spot the gaps before they become a bottleneck, and hand off to your scene builder with confidence.

features.title: Reuse assets across productions
features.subtitle: Duplicated work erodes your asset library
features.content: Pull from a shared library to keep teams from recreating assets that already exist and have been validated. Everyone references the same source of truth.

customerStory.quote: We needed a tool to link tasks and visual deliveries. We wanted practical and pleasant software for the teams. Especially for Filmmakers, who had to access the shots end-to-end at any moment. And of course, all while allowing production managers to track the progress with ease.

header.tagline: Downtime and data loss cost studios money. We make sure neither happens.
header.subtitle: Elite hosting
header.explanation: CGWire's operations team manages your Kitsu installation end to end. Security, uptime, performance, and updates are handled by specialists with decades of cloud infrastructure experience. Your team stays focused on production. We handle everything else.

features.title: Enterprise-grade security, fully managed
features.subtitle: Stop worrying about breaches and compliance
features.content: We adhere to the highest security standards, using only secure transmissions for accessing our up-to-date installations. All secrets are managed through secure vaults, and our employees are screened. A security document is available upon request.

features.title: 99% uptime with proactive monitoring
features.subtitle: Unplanned outages kill deadlines
features.content: Every instance is monitored around the clock. Our team receives alerts before issues escalate and resolves them before they impact your production pipeline.

features.title: Isolated infrastructure, optimized for your location
features.subtitle: Slow tools erode team productivity
features.content: Your instance runs on dedicated resources, isolated from other studios. Servers are provisioned close to your team, and video processing runs on a separate machine so large playlists never slow down your workflow.

features.title: Fully dedicated instance
features.subtitle: Shared infrastructure means unpredictable performance
features.content: Your Kitsu runs on its own infrastructure. No shared resources, no noisy neighbors, no risk of another studio's workload affecting yours. Consistent performance and strict data isolation from day one.

features.title: Updates handled without interruption
features.subtitle: Running outdated software is a security risk
features.content: CGWire's team applies every update and security patch on your behalf. You stay on the latest version without scheduling maintenance windows or pulling engineers away from production work.

header.subtitle: Production Tracker
header.metrics: Over 1,000 productions have already been tracked with Kitsu!
header.explanation: Most production issues are visible before they become critical. Kitsu gives you the scheduling, time tracking, quota monitoring, and budget forecasting tools to catch them early. Get a clear picture of your production at every level, and walk into every stakeholder meeting with accurate data.

features.title: Stop guessing where your production stands
features.subtitle: Gain a 360° view of your data
features.content: Get accurate progress statistics across all your projects, from high-level summaries to task-level detail. Share reports with clients or producers directly from Kitsu with built-in export tools.

features.title: Schedule your work
features.subtitle: Resource conflicts happen when planning is vague
features.content: Define project durations, set milestones, and see how work is distributed across your team. Spot overloads and gaps early so you can adjust before they slow production down.

features.title: Set your expectations
features.subtitle: Productivity issues are invisible without the right data
features.content: Set daily or weekly output targets for your artists and track progress against them. Identify who is blocked and who is ahead so you can act on it, not just report on it.

features.title: See who's working on what
features.subtitle: Coordination gets harder when teams work in silos
features.content: View the full schedule of every artist in your department assigned to your project. See who's available, who's overloaded, and how work is distributed across the team. Make staffing decisions with confidence and keep production moving without surprises.

features.title: Track time spent
features.subtitle: Budget overruns start with hours no one is tracking
features.content: Artists log time directly in Kitsu. You get a clear breakdown by artist, asset, or shot so you always know where your production hours are actually going.

features.title: Anticipate budget overruns
features.subtitle: Financial risk grows when you find out too late
features.content: Compare current spending against your budget at any point in production. Run cost scenarios before making decisions so you can course-correct while you still have options.

customerStory.quote: With Kitsu, people think first about the time saved but what matters more to me is the serenity you get by having everything in order. It gave you more space in your brain to focus on other elements of your production.

header.subtitle: Review Engine
header.metrics: Over 2 000 000 videos were already reviewed with Kitsu!
header.explanation: Feedback cycles are where projects stall. Kitsu centralizes your review process so teams and clients can give precise, contextual feedback on every submission, without losing track of versions, decisions, or history. Less back-and-forth. Fewer missed notes. Faster sign-offs.

features.title: Review consecutive shots
features.subtitle: Stop reviewing shots one email at a time
features.content: Build review playlists and move through all shots in one continuous session. Add comments and annotations without leaving the player. Share playlists directly with clients or run them with your team in real time.

features.title: Compare any version
features.subtitle: Catch what changed between versions
features.content: Place any two versions side by side or stack them as an overlay. Spot regressions, confirm fixes, and validate revisions without hunting through separate files.

features.title: Perform team reviews
features.subtitle: End the problem of misaligned feedback
features.content: Run synchronized review sessions so every participant watches the same frame at the same time. No more conflicting notes from people who reviewed different cuts.

features.title: Share review playlists instantly
features.subtitle: Give clients access without the back-and-forth
features.content: Generate a shareable link for any review playlist and send it to clients, supervisors, or external stakeholders. Everyone reviews the same sequence, with feedback, versions, and approvals kept in one place.

features.title: Annotate directly on frames
features.subtitle: Replace vague notes with precise direction
features.content: Draw, highlight, and write directly on any frame. Annotations are pinned to the specific version and visible to the whole team, so nothing gets lost in translation.

features.title: Full version history
features.subtitle: Never lose track of what was delivered
features.content: Every submitted preview is stored and accessible. Revisit any earlier version to track how a shot evolved, settle disputes, and confirm that previous feedback was addressed.

customerStory.quote: Kitsu has pretty much unified everything and is forcing neater communication not only between artists but from production, management, and clients.

header.tagline: One source of truth for your entire production
header.subtitle: Studio Database
header.metrics: More than 100,000 API calls are made to the Kitsu API every day!
header.explanation: Production data scattered across tools and shared drives kills pipeline efficiency. Kitsu provides a central HTTP API that makes every asset, shot, task, and assignment queryable from any tool in your studio. Your pipeline talks to one place, your artists stay focused on the work.

features.title: Automated workflow
features.subtitle: Stop stitching your pipeline together manually
features.content: When production data lives in one place, everything downstream can be automated. Synchronize data across tools, publish previews directly from your DCC, and trigger custom scripts on any event. What used to require manual coordination runs on its own.

features.title: HTTP API
features.subtitle: Departments working from different data is a production risk
features.content: Assets, shots, casting, task assignments, file locations: every department needs this data, and it needs to be the same data. The Kitsu API gives your entire studio a single queryable source for production information, accessible from any tool, any machine, any location.

features.title: Python client SDK
features.subtitle: Writing boilerplate to query your own production data wastes pipeline time
features.content: The Kitsu Python client gives you intuitive functions to fetch production data without rebuilding request logic every time. A raw client handles complex queries when needed, and a built-in cache keeps response times fast. Install it directly from PyPI and start working.

features.title: Event-driven scripts
features.subtitle: Repetitive pipeline steps that require human input become bottlenecks
features.content: Define what happens when a status changes, a preview is published, or a task is assigned. Your scripts run automatically. Repetitive pipeline steps that used to require someone to notice and act are handled the moment the event fires.

features.title: Chat integrations
features.subtitle: Production updates buried in a tracker no one checks create delays
features.content: Connect Kitsu to Slack or Discord and deliver task updates and production alerts where your team already communicates. Critical information reaches the right people immediately, without anyone having to go look for it.

header.subtitle: Tailor-Made Architecture
header.explanation: Large studios have requirements that standard cloud deployments cannot meet: data sovereignty, network performance, strict access control, and production isolation. CGWire deploys and manages Kitsu directly on your hardware, giving you the security and control of on-premise infrastructure with the same uptime, monitoring, and update guarantees as our cloud. No internal DevOps burden. No compromises on your pipeline.

features.title: On-Premise Installation
features.subtitle: Your data stays on your servers, not ours.
features.content: Studios with strict data residency requirements or latency-sensitive pipelines cannot afford to depend on external cloud infrastructure. We deploy Kitsu directly on your hardware and manage it with the same standards as our cloud: continuous monitoring, regular updates, and guaranteed uptime. Active Directory integration is included so your existing access control policies apply from day one.

features.title: Multi-Instance Setup
features.subtitle: Stop letting productions bleed into each other.
features.content: Running multiple productions on a single instance creates real risks: a pipeline change for one project can break another, and data separation becomes hard to enforce. With isolated Kitsu instances per production, each project runs its own environment. Separate pipelines, separate data, separate access. No interference, no shortcuts.

features.title: Full Infrastructure Stack
features.subtitle: One partner to configure and manage your entire setup.
features.content: Between object storage, video compute, monitoring, and directory services, enterprise deployments involve a lot of moving parts. CGWire configures the complete stack on your private infrastructure so your team does not have to. Everything is set up to work together, and we keep it running.

features.title: Local Video Cluster
features.subtitle: Preview generation is bottlenecking your reviews.
features.content: When your team is reviewing heavy sequences or processing large playlists, waiting on shared cloud resources slows everyone down. A dedicated GPU cluster on your own hardware handles video computing at full speed, on demand, without competing for external capacity.

features.title: Local Object Storage
features.subtitle: Control where your production files actually live.
features.content: Storing production assets and previews in a third-party cloud means accepting their access policies, their retention rules, and their failure modes. With local object storage configured on your infrastructure, your studio owns the data and defines the access. Full control, no dependencies.

header.tagline: Ship faster with fewer coordination failures
header.subtitle: Team Collaboration
header.metrics: More than 5,000,000 comments have already been posted on Kitsu!
header.explanation: Miscommunication between artists, supervisors, and directors is one of the most common causes of rework and missed deadlines. Kitsu centralizes tasks, previews, and feedback in a single workflow so every stakeholder knows what is happening, what needs attention, and what is approved. From single-site studios to distributed teams, everyone works from the same source of truth.

features.title: Tasks, comments and previews
features.subtitle: Stop losing feedback in emails and chat threads
features.content: Assign tasks to artists and have them publish their work directly in Kitsu when ready for review. Producers, supervisors, and directors iterate on the same platform, with every comment and version tracked in context. Real-time notifications mean no one is waiting on information they should already have.

features.title: Todos
features.subtitle: Artists who do not know their priorities block production
features.content: Each artist sees exactly what is assigned to them, in what order, and at what stage. They can update progress without chasing a supervisor for instructions. Less back-and-forth means more time on the work that matters.

features.title: Daily Supervision Tasks
features.subtitle: Supervisors need a clear overview without jumping between pages
features.content: Supervisors can instantly access a department-wide view of production through the Daily Supervision Tasks page. From any page in Kitsu, simply click on a task type name to see an overview of all related tasks across your department. This makes it easy to monitor progress, identify bottlenecks, review priorities, and ensure work keeps moving without manually collecting updates from artists.

features.title: Newsfeed
features.subtitle: Problems caught late cost more to fix
features.content: The newsfeed surfaces every comment, status change, and new preview across the production in real time. Supervisors and producers spot issues early, before they compound. Nothing falls through the cracks because someone missed a message.

features.title: Slack and Discord notifications
features.subtitle: Relevant updates get buried when they live in the wrong tool
features.content: Route Kitsu activity into the channels your team already monitors. Comments, status changes, and new previews appear directly in Slack or Discord so the right people respond without having to check a separate platform.

customerStory.quote: A few days are enough to understand all the features required to run a production. Everyone in the studio starts quickly, especially artists who really enjoy Kitsu. We didn't notice this enthusiasm with the previous tools we used.

header.subtitle: Top-Notch Support
header.explanation: Production problems do not wait for business hours. Get answers in under 30 minutes via in-app chat, from a team of former production managers who know your workflows firsthand. Every conversation is saved and tracked, so nothing falls through the cracks.

features.title: Fast response time
features.subtitle: Stop waiting days for a reply
features.content: We respond in 30 minutes on average. Whether you need help finding a specific menu or want to rethink a workflow, you get a clear answer fast so your team can keep moving.

features.title: Support when you need it
features.subtitle: Reach us where it works for you
features.content: Access support directly in Kitsu, on Discord, or over Zoom. Whatever the issue, we are available through the channel that fits your situation.

features.title: Assisted by experts
features.subtitle: Talk to people who have done your job
features.content: Our support team is made up of production veterans who have managed real pipelines. They understand the pressure you are under, speak your language, and help you get the most out of Kitsu.

features.title: Track your requests
features.subtitle: No issue gets forgotten or ignored
features.content: Every support conversation is saved and can be escalated through an integrated ticketing system. You always know where things stand, from first contact to resolution.

features.title: Shape the product with us
features.subtitle: Your pipeline needs influence the roadmap
features.content: When Kitsu does not cover a specific need, we do not just log a ticket. We work with you to scope and build the feature. Your production requirements directly shape what gets built next.

ftrack.title: An open-source alternative to Ftrack
ftrack.subtitle: Open-source production tracker for animation and VFX studios, ready from day one.

sections.title: Who Kitsu is for
content: Kitsu is used by animation and VFX studios from 10 to 500 users, on TV series, feature films, shorts, and video game productions. Productions often run with distributed teams or with several studios collaborating on the same show.

Ftrack also targets animation and VFX studios, with a stronger footprint on mid-to-large facilities running customized pipelines. The two tools overlap on the core mission of production tracking, with different bets on how to get there.

sections.title: What Kitsu covers
content: A production tracker covers a well-known set of needs: sequences, shots, assets, tasks, statuses, schedules, time tracking, reviews, casting, permissions, a documented API, and DCC integrations. Kitsu covers this scope, the same as Ftrack does. The difference is in how the pieces are presented and how the studio interacts with them.

One area where Ftrack pushes further is its DCC integration footprint via Ftrack Connect. Kitsu's integration story is leaner and relies on the open-source ecosystem: dedicated tools like Prism or Ayon integrate with Kitsu and cover the deep DCC pipeline work, while Kitsu stays focused on the tracking and review workflow.

sections.title: Approach to configuration
content: Ftrack is highly configurable: entities, workflow, fields, and UI can be tailored to each studio's pipeline. That depth fits teams with dedicated pipeline engineers who can model and maintain a custom setup.

Kitsu takes the opposite bet. A production tracker is most useful when the whole studio shares one reading of the work, not when each team adapts the tool to its preferences. Standardization is the feature: the workflow model (sequences, shots, assets, tasks, statuses) is fixed, the UI is the same for every role, and the standard configuration works out of the box. Artists, supervisors, production, and IT all use the same interface from day one.
Variations between productions are handled at the project level: task types, statuses, asset categories, custom metadata, schedules. No UI rebuild, no consultants between the studio and the work.
Onboarding reflects this difference. With Kitsu, new artists are productive within a day. With Ftrack, the customization phase typically precedes the productivity phase.

sections.title: Support and roadmap shaped by the people who use it
content: Support is handled by industry professionals (former pipeline and production roles), based in Paris. Bugs and questions get answered in hours, not days.

The roadmap lives on a public Canny board where studios vote on what we build next. Current priorities (the permissions system, software integrations) come from there. Decisions are visible, and they can be discussed in the open before they ship.
An active Discord runs alongside, where the CGWire team participates daily. It is the same channel the team uses for incident heads-up, release notes, and discussion of upcoming work.
Ftrack support is handled through the editor's channels. Roadmap and product decisions are internal to the company.

sections.title: Open source and transparency
content: Kitsu is open source (AGPL, repository at github.com/cgwire/kitsu). The code can be read, audited, forked, and deployed by anyone. The changelog and the roadmap are public, so studios can see what is coming, what is in progress, and what is being decided.
For teams that want long-term visibility on the tools they depend on, this matters: no surprise pivot, no surprise pricing, no closed-door product decision.
CGWire is an independent company, five people based in Paris, with no external investors. The choices we make on Kitsu (open source, public roadmap, pricing) are ours to keep.
Ftrack is proprietary software, owned by Backbone. The roadmap and codebase stay inside the editor.

sections.title: Self-hosted, real and free
content: Around 200 studios run their own Kitsu instance today. Deployment is a Docker compose against a standard PostgreSQL database, which pipeline TDs and IT teams can query and back up with their usual tools.

Ftrack offers an on-premise deployment option as a paid add-on, separate from the standard subscription. With Kitsu, the self-hosted path is free, documented, and at the same feature level as the cloud build.

For studios subject to GDPR, IP protection clauses, or data residency requirements (common on European or Japanese productions), keeping the full stack inside your own infrastructure is a regular deployment, not a special tier.

sections.title: Where the two tools differ
content: A few areas where the two tools differ, item by item:

items: Casting and breakdown: native and integrated in Kitsu, with dedicated views. Ftrack handles breakdown through asset relationships, which gives flexibility but requires more setup.

Quotas and timesheets: Kitsu ties them directly to schedules and task types, useful for studios planning charges per artist. Ftrack offers time tracking; quota planning typically requires additional configuration.
API and integrations: Kitsu exposes a REST API with a documented Python client (gazu). Ftrack uses an event-driven API with its own SDK. Both work, the learning curve differs.

Review engine: Kitsu offers full playlists with quick access to all versions or shareable playlist links (send a single URL, the reviewer can comment without an account). Ftrack Review is a strong tool with its own ecosystem (cineSync), oriented toward higher-end remote review.

content: Ftrack's broader DCC integration footprint (Maya, Nuke, Houdini, etc.) is a recognized strength. Kitsu's integration story is leaner and focused on the everyday tracking workflow rather than deep DCC plumbing.

sections.title: Cost
content: License pricing sits in the same range. The differences sit around hosting, source code, and how the roadmap is governed.

content.caption: Kitsu vs Ftrack, at a glance
rows: ~€25 to €35 per user per month, depending on plan

content: Ftrack pricing varies by plan and region, so the numbers above are an order of magnitude. Studios doing a serious comparison should pull a current quote from ftrack.com.

items.q: Ftrack is very configurable. Isn't a fixed workflow limiting?
items.a: Configurability has a cost: it postpones the moment when the team becomes productive. Standardization also pays off in everyday collaboration: a fixed structure means no 'where do I post the retake?' moments, the same shape applies everywhere, and a new artist or coordinator picks up where the last one left off. Kitsu's standard workflow is the result of years of iteration with studios and is flexible enough to cover 2D, 3D, shorts, feature films, series, video games, VR, AR, and stop motion. Most productions fit it without modification. Studios that need a deeply custom pipeline are usually better served by Ftrack or by combining Kitsu with a pipeline-specific tool like Prism or Ayon.

items.q: Ftrack Review is a strong tool. Does Kitsu cover the same ground?
items.a: Kitsu has a built-in review engine with version comparison, drawing tools, and shareable playlist links that let external reviewers comment without an account. Ftrack Review remains a recognized tool with its own ecosystem (cineSync) for higher-end remote review.

items.q: What about DCC integrations? Ftrack Connect is comprehensive.
items.a: Kitsu exposes a REST API with a documented Python client (gazu). For deep DCC pipeline work, Prism and Ayon integrate with Kitsu and cover the territory Ftrack Connect addresses. Many studios run that combination today.

items.q: Open source sounds risky for a production-critical tool.
items.a: Kitsu has been in active development since 2016, used in production by hundreds of studios. Cloud customers get direct support from the CGWire team. Studios on self-hosted can subscribe to the Partners program for a direct line to the team. The codebase being open means you can audit it, fork it, and you are not locked into a single vendor.

items.q: Migration during an active production is too risky.
items.a: We support studios on running both tools in parallel during the transition. The data export and schema mapping are well understood, and we can adapt the pace to your production calendar.

sections.title: Migrating from Ftrack
content: Moving from Ftrack to Kitsu is a path we know well. We can support you on data export, schema mapping, history preservation, and running both tools in parallel during the transition. Get in touch and we'll walk through what your migration would look like.

shotgrid.title: An open-source alternative to Autodesk Flow Production Tracking
shotgrid.subtitle: Open-source production tracker for animation and VFX studios. Ready to use for the whole team, from day one.

sections.title: Who Kitsu is for
content: Kitsu is used by animation and VFX studios from 10 to 500 users, on TV series, feature films, shorts, and video game productions. Productions often run with distributed teams, or with several studios collaborating on the same show.

Autodesk Flow (formerly ShotGrid) primarily targets very large VFX facilities such as ILM, Weta, or Disney. The two tools overlap, but with different centers of gravity. Picking the right one usually starts with this question.

sections.title: What Kitsu covers
content: A production tracker covers a well-known set of needs: sequences, shots, assets, tasks, statuses, schedules, time tracking, reviews, casting, permissions, a documented API, and DCC integrations. Kitsu covers this scope, the same as ShotGrid does. The difference is in how the pieces are presented and how the studio interacts with them.

One exception is worth naming: ShotGrid Toolkit, the deep DCC pipeline layer that ships with ShotGrid. Kitsu takes a different approach: pipeline integration is delegated to dedicated open-source tools that integrate with Kitsu, such as Prism or Ayon. They focus exclusively on DCC pipeline work and typically go further than a tracker-bundled module would.

sections.title: Approach to configuration
content: ShotGrid offers deep entity-level configuration: entities, workflows, fields, and UI can be tailored to each studio's pipeline. That depth fits very large facilities with dedicated pipeline engineers who can model and maintain a custom setup over years.

Kitsu takes the opposite bet. A production tracker is most useful when the whole studio shares one reading of the work, not when each team adapts the tool to its preferences. Standardization is the feature: the workflow model (sequences, shots, assets, tasks, statuses) is fixed, the UI is the same for every role, and the standard configuration works out of the box. Artists, supervisors, production, and IT all use the same interface from day one.
Variations between productions are handled at the project level: task types, statuses, asset categories, custom metadata, schedules. No UI rebuild, no consultants between the studio and the work.
Onboarding reflects this difference. With Kitsu, new artists are productive within a day. With ShotGrid, the customization phase typically precedes the productivity phase.

sections.title: What it actually costs
content: License pricing is broadly similar between Kitsu Cloud and ShotGrid: around €30 per user per month for the base plan, around €50 with support included. What changes the total cost of ownership is the work needed to make the tool fit your studio.

content.caption: Kitsu Cloud vs ShotGrid, item by item
rows: €30 per user per month (€50 with support)
Several consultant days, valued at market rate
Internal training on a customized version
Included cloud, or self-hosted for free
Cloud only since the end of standard on-premise

content: License costs land in the same ballpark. The gap with ShotGrid widens as the studio grows, driven by setup, custom development, and ongoing maintenance rather than the license itself.

sections.title: Where the two tools differ
content: A few areas where the two tools differ, item by item:

items: Casting and breakdown: native and integrated in Kitsu, with dedicated views. ShotGrid typically handles breakdown through entity relationships and custom fields, which requires more setup.

Quotas and timesheets: Kitsu ties them directly to schedules and task types, useful for studios planning charges per artist. ShotGrid offers time logs and reporting; quota planning typically requires custom configuration or third-party add-ons.

API and integrations: Kitsu exposes a REST API with a documented Python client (gazu). ShotGrid uses a proprietary Python API, well known in the VFX world but tied to the Autodesk ecosystem. Both work, the integration path differs.

Review engine: Kitsu offers shareable playlist links (send a single URL, the reviewer can comment without a Kitsu account). ShotGrid Review handles versions and notes inside the application; external review typically goes through related tools.

content: ShotGrid's footprint in very large facilities, including its event-driven architecture and deep customization layer, is a recognized strength. Kitsu's product stays focused on the everyday tracking workflow rather than full pipeline customization.

sections.title: Hosting and data ownership
content: Autodesk has discontinued its standard on-premise option for ShotGrid. Self-hosted deployments still exist, but go through a long approval process and carry a significant additional cost. The codebase remains proprietary, so the future of features is decided inside Autodesk.

Kitsu is open source. The code can be audited line by line, deployed on your servers, and operated autonomously. Around 200 studios run their own Kitsu instance today, in parallel with our hosted offering.

For studios subject to GDPR, IP protection clauses, or data residency requirements (common on European or Japanese productions), keeping the full stack inside your own infrastructure is a regular deployment, not a special tier.
The cloud version stays available for studios that prefer managed hosting, with the same feature set as the open-source build.

sections.title: Community and transparency
content: The codebase, roadmap, and changelog are public. Bug reports and feature requests are tracked on GitHub issues and on a public Canny board where studios vote on what to build next. Our Discord is active, with the CGWire team answering within the day on most threads.
The team is made of industry professionals (former pipeline and production roles), based in Paris. Support is handled by people who have worked on productions, not a generic tier-one queue.
CGWire is an independent company, five people based in Paris, with no external investors. The choices we make on Kitsu (open source, public roadmap, pricing) are ours to keep.

items.q: Kitsu doesn't have entity-level customization like ShotGrid. Isn't that a limitation?
items.a: It is a deliberate choice. ShotGrid's customization layer fits very large facilities with dedicated pipeline engineers. For everyone else, it is a setup cost that delays getting the team productive. Standardization also pays off in everyday collaboration: a fixed structure means no 'where do I post the retake?' moments, the same shape applies everywhere, and a new artist or coordinator picks up where the last one left off. Kitsu's standard workflow is the result of years of iteration with studios and is flexible enough to cover 2D, 3D, shorts, feature films, series, video games, VR, AR, and stop motion. Most productions fit it without modification.

items.q: What about the DCC integrations we rely on?
items.a: Kitsu exposes a REST API and ships a documented Python client (gazu) that makes building integrations with Maya, Nuke, Houdini, Blender, and others straightforward. For deeper pipeline work, Prism and Ayon are dedicated open-source tools that integrate with Kitsu and cover the ground ShotGrid Toolkit covers, with a sharper focus.

items.q: Is Kitsu only for small studios?
items.a: Kitsu is used from 10 to 500 users per instance, on TV series, feature films, shorts, and games. Several customers run productions of hundreds of artists on Kitsu today, and larger studios often deploy one instance per project to scale comfortably to much higher company-wide headcounts.

items.q: We have years of ShotGrid data. Is migration realistic?
items.a: Yes. Moving a production database with thousands of shots, assets, versions, and notes is well within scope. We can support you on data export, schema mapping, and history preservation.

items.q: Open source means no commercial support, right?
items.a: No. Kitsu Cloud customers get direct support from the CGWire team, the same people who build the product. Studios on self-hosted can subscribe to the Partners program for a direct line to the team.

sections.title: Migrating from ShotGrid
content: Moving from ShotGrid to Kitsu is a path we know well. We can support you on data export, schema mapping, history preservation, and running both tools in parallel during the transition. Get in touch and we'll walk through what your migration would look like.

quote.text: Kitsu has pretty much unified everything and is forcing neater communication.
quote.author: Pete Draper, Head of VFX at Makuta VFX (RRR movie)

panels.title: For Producers
descriptions: The producer has a comprehensive view of the production. They can verify any information from the client or the contractor.

Kitsu provides financial insights into the production. It shows where money is being spent and why. The producer can identify which shots or assets are the most costly, and use charts to always provide an up-to-date overview of the production's progress.
The producer can independently monitor the progress of the show. No one needs to pause their work to provide pictures or previews. They can view the progress of an episode in real-time.

panels.title: For Production Managers
descriptions: As a Production Manager, tracking production costs and knowing the status of reviews and retakes is essential.
Kitsu provides efficient timesheets where you can see the number of hours spent by each artist, per day, per week, or per month for all productions.
For each shot or asset, you can see the overall time spent on it, helping to identify entities that cost too much. You can set estimates for every task and compare them to the time spent.
With the statistics page, you can create efficient production reports. No need to chase down information; it shows you the production progress in real-time.
You don't need to spend time gathering files for the daily or weekly review. With the playlist page, you can create reviews quickly without missing anything.

panels.title: For Directors
descriptions: Directors need an efficient way to review pending tasks and stay aware of production progress. This enables them to react quickly and prevent the team from wasting time due to missed feedback.

With Kitsu's playlist system, the Director can review multiple shots from anywhere, whether it's a few pending reviews or the entire movie. Kitsu includes editing functions like changing the shot order or switching versions on the fly, allowing the Director to be more precise about what they want to see.

panels.title: For Supervisors
descriptions: Supervisors need instant access to information at all times. They don't want to spend time searching for the right spreadsheet or file to comment on.
Fortunately, with Kitsu, everything is in one place. Supervisors don't have to wait for others to know what to do. They always know what to review and which retakes need to be done.
Task assignments, mentions, and comments ensure that information is efficiently sent to the right person. Supervisors have everything at hand to make the right decision.

panels.title: For Artists
descriptions: Artists need quick and easy access to their tasks and a simple way to communicate about their work by posting comments and previews.
When an artist logs in, they can access their to-do list. At a glance, they can see all relevant information about what needs to be done.
All tasks contain detailed metadata, such as due dates, time estimates, priority level, current status, and comment history.

With a single click, the artist opens a comment panel without leaving their to-do list. It's super fast to respond or send a new preview. Through the notification system (in-app, email, or Slack), they know when something happens.

reasons.title: Why you should use Kitsu for your Feature Film Production
items: Get an overview of the progress of all your sequences
Run full sequence reviews through a single playlist with external collaborators
Unify communication with all vendors

quote.text: Kitsu is the exact solution we were looking for to manage our students projects.
quote.author: Carlos de Carvalho, Director of studies at Pôle 3D

panels.title: For a Student
descriptions: Students need a central place to collaborate with each other and their teachers. Kitsu provides a dedicated workspace for sharing information.

Kitsu provides a framework for students to organize and receive feedback from their teachers. Thanks to the notification system (in-app, email, or Slack), they are always informed about any required changes.
Your students will learn how to use a production tracker utilized in real studios. They will gain an early understanding of prioritization and the basics of professional communication.

panels.title: For a Teacher
descriptions: Teachers can stay focused on their areas of expertise. They won't miss any requests for feedback or new previews. They will also be able to identify which student groups require assistance.
Movie reviews are seamless thanks to the playlist module, which allows comparing different versions at different stages of production.
Kitsu serves as a dedicated place to store the entire project history, capturing how decisions were made.

panels.title: For an Academic Director
descriptions: Academic Directors can monitor final student projects, easily understand how assignments are progressing, and quickly identify issues.

Academic Directors can quickly determine the status of a project and evaluate the appropriateness of task assignments. Through the schedules, they will have a comprehensive overview of the major milestones of the project.

reasons.title: Why you should use Kitsu for your Education Program
items: Preparing students for industry-standard production management
Playlist makes reviews easy for teachers
Efficient student interactions with built-in comment system
