quote.text: Kitsu is a very artist-friendly tool for planning and tracking your project. The UI is clean and easy to understand. We received a lot of positive feedback from the team.
quote.author: Christian Zehetmeier, Head of Production at Woodblock

panels.title: For Production Managers
descriptions: As a Production Manager, you need to track production costs and know what has been reviewed and how many times it required a retake.
Kitsu provides efficient timesheets where you can see the number of hours spent per artist over a day, week, or month. You can access the details by production.
For each shot or asset, you can see the overall time spent on it. This helps identify entities that are too costly. You can set estimates for each task and compare them to the actual time spent.

The statistics page allows you to write your producer report faster. You don't have to chase down information. You can simply report the data and track the progress of the project throughout its lifetime.
You don't need to spend time gathering files for daily or weekly reviews. With the playlist page, you can set up reviews with a single click and ensure nothing is missed.

panels.title: For Directors
descriptions: Directors need an efficient way to review pending tasks and stay informed about production progress. This allows them to react quickly and prevent the team from wasting time due to incorrect directions.

To achieve this, Kitsu provides a playlist system, allowing the Director to review multiple shots consecutively. This can include pending reviews or the entire movie. Kitsu also offers editing functions such as changing the shot order or switching versions on the fly. This enables the Director to be more precise about what they want to see.
The Director can monitor activities via the news feed, allowing for quick reactions when something goes wrong. There is no need to wait until the end of the week to respond.

panels.title: For Supervisors
descriptions: Supervisors work on multiple productions simultaneously. They need to act quickly and stay up to date.
Thanks to its real-time capabilities, Kitsu always displays the latest data. Additionally, dashboards and task lists emphasize the most important information.
Supervisors can save their most-used filters, making it easy for them to find their retakes or pending reviews.
Task assignments, mentions, and comments ensure that information is always sent to the right person, giving supervisors everything they need to make informed decisions.

panels.title: For Artists
descriptions: Artists need quick access to their tasks and a simple way to communicate about their work by posting comments and previews.
When artists log in, they land on their to-do list. At a glance, they see which tasks to work on. Related data is displayed: entity, task type, estimation, due date, and the most recent comment.

reasons.title: Why you should use Kitsu for your Short Movie production
items: Communicate easily with your Artists and keep the history
Schedule and manage several projects at the same time
Send reviews and final projects through the client gateway

quote.text: When you work on a 52x11' series, you have to deal with an insane amount of deliveries. Fortunately, with Kitsu, everything was running smoothly.
quote.author: Cécile Hergaux, Head of Studio at Cube Creative

panels.title: For Producers
descriptions: The producer has a comprehensive view of the production. They can verify any information from the client or the contractor.

Kitsu allows the producer to see the financial status of the production, including where money is spent and why. They can identify the most costly shots or assets. Thanks to the charts, they always know how the production is progressing.
The producer doesn't depend on others to see the progress of the show. No one has to stop working to provide updates. The producer can independently view the progress of a sequence in real-time.

panels.title: For Production Managers
descriptions: As a Production Manager, you need to track production costs and know what has been reviewed and how many retakes have occurred.
Kitsu provides efficient timesheets where you can see the number of hours spent by each artist per day, week, or month for all productions.
For each shot or asset, you can see the overall time spent on it. This helps identify entities that are too costly. You can set estimates for each task and compare them to the actual time spent.
The statistics page allows you to write your producer report faster. You don't have to chase down information. It shows you the production's progress in real-time.
You don't need to spend time gathering files for daily or weekly reviews. With the playlist page, you can set up reviews quickly and ensure nothing is missed.

panels.title: For Directors
descriptions: Directors have an efficient way to review pending tasks and stay aware of what's happening in production. This allows them to react quickly and avoid the team wasting time due to incorrect directions.

Kitsu provides a playlist system: the Director can review multiple shots consecutively, whether pending reviews or the entire movie. Kitsu includes editing functions such as changing the shot order or switching versions on the fly, allowing the Director to be more precise about what they want to see.

panels.title: For Supervisors
descriptions: Supervisors need instant access to information at all times. They don't want to search for the right spreadsheet or file to comment on.
Fortunately, with Kitsu, everything is in one place. Supervisors don't have to wait for others to know what to do. They always know what to review and which retakes need to be done.
Task assignments, mentions, and comments ensure that information is always sent to the right person efficiently. Ultimately, supervisors have everything at hand to make and share the right decisions.

panels.title: For Artists
descriptions: Artists need quick access to their tasks and a simple way to communicate about their work by posting comments and previews.
When artists log in, they land on their to-do list. At a glance, they see what tasks they need to complete.
All tasks are properly detailed: due date, estimation, priority level, current status, and the latest comment.

With a single click, artists can open a comment panel without leaving their to-do list. It's very fast to respond or send a new preview. Through the notification system (in-app, email, or Slack), they know when something happens.

reasons.title: Why you should use Kitsu for your TV Series production
items: Get an overview of the progress of all your episodes
Run full episode reviews through a single playlist with external collaborators
Keep track of retake numbers and build report easily

quote.text: Every time I learn about another feature of Kitsu, it becomes a more powerful tool. It's like Kitsu is full of surprises. It's been a robust resource that I know I'll continue to learn, adapt and expand upon, so it always has more to offer!
quote.author: Ariel McKay , Production Assistant at MDHR

panels.title: For Supervisors
descriptions: Supervisors need instant access to information at all times. They don't want to search for the right spreadsheet or file to comment on.
With Kitsu, everything is in one place. Supervisors don't have to wait for others to know what to do. They always know what to review and can easily send their feedback.
With the playlist system, they can review everything in a row. They can do it alone or invite the whole team to the review.
Task assignments, mentions, and comments ensure that information is always sent to the right person efficiently. Ultimately, supervisors have everything at hand to make and share the right decisions.

panels.title: For Artists
descriptions: Artists need quick access to their tasks and a simple way to communicate about their work by posting comments and previews.
Via the Kitsu todo-list, they can report their progress efficiently and push their deliveries. They get timesheets to declare the amount of hours they spent working for your studio.
All tasks are properly detailed. Artists get clear feedback and know when their work is validated.
They have many views to manage their workload: list, kanban and calendar and can see the whole production to understand the context.
Through the notification system (in-app, email, or Slack), they always know when something happens.

panels.title: For Project Managers
descriptions: Kitsu provides efficient task management and scheduling features. It allows you to set proper workloads for every artist and have a global perspective on your project.
For each asset, you can see the overall effort spent on it. This helps identify elements that are too costly. Then, you can access to the whole history and identify bottlenecks.
You can also track the time billed by your freelancers through a simple timesheet system. All the data can be aggregated at week and month level.

reasons.title: Why you should use Kitsu for your video games production
items: Build a library of all your in-game assets and version them
Optimized design for artists to get clear directives and send their deliveries
Collaboration features dedicated to the creative team, Kitsu is like Jira for artists

ejt.name: Promenons-nous dans les bois / Studio EJT-labo

wizz-1.quote: Kitsu gives us sovereignty over our data. Our whole pipeline relies on it.

wizz-2.quote: You don't need to use every Kitsu feature to get value from it.

wizz-3.quote: Wizz primarily uses Kitsu as a visualization and tracking layer, and that alone delivers significant benefit to coordinators and directors.

wizz-4.quote: Kitsu's value compounds as it becomes the authoritative data source for downstream tools.

wizz-5.quote: Anything you record in Kitsu can be used to configure, validate, and automate work elsewhere in the pipeline.

normaal-1.quote: For production tracking, the team chose Kitsu.

normaal-2.quote: You cannot anticipate all the needs you will encounter. Using a tool like Kitsu which has already been tested and developed helps you, because most of the use cases you will have to deal with have already been solved.

normaal-3.quote: When a shot is assigned to an artist, the pipeline can read the breakdown to determine which characters and sets appear in that shot, then pre-sync all the relevant files to that artist's workstation before they even open the task.

normaal-4.quote: Automating our pipeline meant choosing tools like Kitsu that had already absorbed years of community knowledge, so the team could focus on the problems that were actually unique to the production.

woodblock.quote: Kitsu is a very artist-friendly tool for planning and tracking your project. The UI is clean and easy to understand. We received a lot of positive feedback from the team.

mdhr.quote: Every time I learn about another feature of Kitsu, it becomes a more powerful tool. It's like Kitsu is full of surprises. It's been a robust resource that I know I'll continue to learn, adapt and expand upon, so it always has more to offer!

cube.quote: When you work on a 52x11' series, you have to deal with an insane amount of deliveries. Fortunately, with Kitsu, everything was running smoothly.

pole3d.quote: Kitsu is the exact solution we were looking for to manage our students projects.

tant-mieux.quote: Communication with remote studios have been simplified. We can discuss a specific asset or task without exchanging 10 emails. If we have any doubts, we can rely on what is indicated on the platform.

miyu.quote: A few days are enough to understand all the features required to run a production. Everyone in the studio starts quickly, especially artists who really enjoy Kitsu. We didn't notice this enthusiasm with the previous tools we used.

miyu-2.quote: The first and most important strength of Kitsu is simplicity.

miyu-3.quote: Kitsu made the bet to start accessible and add complexity only when users ask for it.

miyu-4.quote: The most capable tool in the world has zero value if the team refuses to use it. Kitsu is built for artist adoption.

miyu-5.quote: A tool that requires a specialist to run it and gets avoided by everyone else is not, in practice, a working tool.

miyu-6.quote: Kitsu's architecture built around the concepts of shots and assets is flexible enough to accommodate genuinely unusual workflows.

miyu-7.quote: Kitsu gives all teams real-time visibility into each other's progress.

miyu-8.quote: Within 30 minutes, the project was fully restored because Kitsu's event log captures all modifications in real time.

fost.quote: With Kitsu, people think first about the time saved but what matters more to me is the serenity you get by having everything in order. It gave you more space in your brain to focus on other elements of your production.

fost-2.quote: We switched to Kitsu in 2020. Spoiler: we never went back.

fost-3.quote: Kitsu isn't just a production tracking tool but also the central data layer for the entire studio pipeline.

fost-4.quote: Kitsu is the single source of truth that every department and every tool can read from and write to.

fost-5.quote: The Kitsu API and documentation aren't a nightmare to navigate, which is rare for tools in the industry.

fost-6.quote: The result of adopting Kitsu is a studio that scaled from 60 to 150 people on a single production and kept the ship on course.

fost-7.quote: This kind of ongoing collaboration between a studio and a software vendor is uncommon in animation production software. It reflects both the open-source nature of Kitsu and the fact that CGWire's business model depends on studios genuinely succeeding with the tool.

adm.quote: We needed a tool to link tasks and visual deliveries. We wanted practical and pleasant software for the teams. Especially for Filmmakers, who had to access the shots end-to-end at any moment. And of course, all while allowing production managers to track the progress with ease.

makuta.quote: Kitsu has pretty much unified everything and is forcing neater communication not only between artists but from production, management, and clients.

ryff.quote: Kitsu was straightforward. It was simple. You didn't need a degree in computer science like you do with alternatives.

ryff-2.quote: Kitsu is straightforward. It's simple. Not just for the production guys, but also for the artists as well. You don't need a degree in computer science to use it.

ryff-3.quote: A production tracker only works if everyone uses it, and that means it has to be approachable for artists, not just supervisors. Kitsu passed that test from day one.

remembers.quote: I knew that if I brought in a new tool, I needed to address a need that hadn't been identified by the core team already in place. The main challenge right away was not to constrain them.

remembers-2.quote: Kitsu is something that's very, very easy to pick up. You can click anywhere, you see the film's images, you see all the departments and sequences that might relate to your own.

remembers-3.quote: It's also enjoyable and motivating to browse around in Kitsu. It's not 'oh no, I have to post my latest version.'

remembers-4.quote: You can understand that between Kitsu and using a spreadsheet, we've already made an enormous step forward as a studio. And it's not just about productivity, it's more enjoyable too.

remembers-5.quote: The trial demo reassured me greatly. We were obviously looking for something very intuitive, because since I was addressing people who weren't asking for tools, they needed to be able to pick it up and get on board very naturally.

remembers-6.quote: Kitsu makes cross-department communication quick and easy. Even just small edits, things that slipped through because the shots had already been approved.

remembers-7.quote: All the valuable data for production tracking already exists in Kitsu.

cube-1.quote: Kitsu is the project's collective memory.

cube-2.quote: Kitsu knows the status of every asset, every shot, and the overall state of production.

cube-3.quote: For Cube, Kitsu is the single source of truth.

cube-4.quote: With Kitsu, artists do not need to guess where something is.

cube-5.quote: Kitsu helps tremendously with infrastructure and extensibility, thanks to its flexible architecture and open-source nature.

tetsuo-1.quote: You can have a meeting with multiple Kitsu users that are all on the same playlist and they can all draw on the same thing. That means you can talk about very specific things at a very specific time.

tetsuo-2.quote: Once we can get everyone involved to give visual feedback, we get team ownership of the shots and we build a communication tool, a language that the whole team shares.

tetsuo-3.quote: Invite everyone to draw on the frame. Invite your clients, invite your stakeholders.

tant-mieux.quote: Communication with remote studios have been simplified. We can discuss a specific asset or task without exchanging 10 emails. If we have any doubts, we can rely on what is indicated on the platform.

miyu.quote: A few days are enough to understand all the features required to run a production. Everyone in the studio starts quickly, especially artists who really enjoy Kitsu. We didn't notice this enthusiasm with the previous tools we used.

fost.quote: With Kitsu, people think first about the time saved but what matters more to me is the serenity you get by having everything in order. It gave you more space in your brain to focus on other elements of your production.

adm.quote: We needed a tool to link tasks and visual deliveries. We wanted practical and pleasant software for the teams. Especially for Filmmakers, who had to access the shots end-to-end at any moment. And of course, all while allowing production managers to track the progress with ease.

makuta.quote: Kitsu has pretty much unified everything and is forcing neater communication not only between artists but from production, management, and clients.

ryff.quote: Kitsu was straightforward. It was simple. You didn't need a degree in computer science like you do with alternatives.

remembers.quote: I knew that if I brought in a new tool, I needed to address a need that hadn't been identified by the core team already in place. The main challenge right away was not to constrain them.
